cards.altText: Subaru Baja EJ255 engine - petrol 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - multi-layer steel gaskets recommended on high-mileage turbo units.
Turbocharger wear - boost loss and oil consumption common after 80k miles.
Oil consumption - piston ring wear on high-mileage examples.

cards.altText: Subaru Baja EJ251 engine - petrol 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - most common failure point on EJ25 series boxer engines.
Oil consumption - piston ring wear increases oil use on higher-mileage units.
Timing belt service critical - interference engine, belt failure causes catastrophic damage.

cards.altText: Subaru Baja EJ252 engine - petrol 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - upgraded multi-layer steel gaskets recommended during rebuild.
Valve cover gasket leaks - common on ageing SOHC boxer engines.
Oil consumption - piston ring wear on units over 100k miles.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru Baja Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru Baja's EJ25-series boxer engines are robust but share common failure points with Legacy and Outback donors. Head gasket failure dominates replacement enquiries, followed by turbo issues on EJ255 units. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded multi-layer steel head gaskets and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Why Subaru Baja EJ25 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Baja Base, Sport, Turbo, Turbo Limited (2002-2006, EJ251/EJ252/EJ255)
problems.rootCause: Original single-layer composite gaskets degrade under heat cycles and boxer engine vibration, allowing coolant/oil cross-contamination.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace gaskets with upgraded MLS units, reseal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/heads undamaged; 30-50k mile expectation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EJ25 engine with MLS gaskets, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; 12-month unlimited warranty, addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: On a Subaru Baja worth £3,000-£8,000, a full rebuilt engine often represents better value than a head gasket-only repair, especially if mileage exceeds 100k. We recommend rebuilt units with upgraded MLS gaskets to prevent repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EJ25 engine prices for your Subaru Baja

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - EJ255 Baja Turbo Boost Loss & Oil Consumption
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Baja Turbo, Turbo Limited (2005-2006, EJ255)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearings and seals wear under heat and boost pressure, causing oil leakage into intake/exhaust and loss of boost.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, rebuild or replace with reconditioned unit, replace lines/gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block healthy; 40-60k mile expectation on turbo

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EJ255 Turbo Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EJ255 with new/reconditioned turbo, MLS gaskets, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; addresses turbo + common EJ255 failure points together

problems.recommendation: If your Baja Turbo has high mileage or shows signs of oil consumption beyond the turbo, a full rebuilt EJ255 engine often costs less than separate turbo + head gasket repairs. We recommend rebuilt units with turbo health guarantees.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EJ255 turbo engine prices for your Subaru Baja

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - High-Mileage EJ25 Piston Ring Wear
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Baja Base, Sport, Turbo, Turbo Limited (2002-2006, EJ251/EJ252/EJ255)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring lands wear on ageing boxer engines, allowing oil to pass into combustion chambers and burn.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip engine, replace piston rings, hone cylinders, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Complex labour; viable only if block/heads in excellent condition

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt engine with updated piston rings, MLS gaskets, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective long-term solution; eliminates oil consumption root cause

problems.recommendation: For Baja owners experiencing excessive oil use, a rebuilt engine with updated piston rings typically offers better value than a ring-only repair, especially on grey import vehicles where labour costs are high.
problems.cta: Get quotes for low-oil-consumption rebuilt Baja engines

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Damage Risk on EJ25 Interference Engines
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Baja Base, Sport, Turbo, Turbo Limited (2002-2006, all EJ25 codes)
problems.rootCause: Timing belt wears or snaps due to age, mileage, or tensioner failure; EJ25 is an interference engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump; preventative only
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done before belt fails

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine After Belt Failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine after valve/piston damage from belt failure
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after catastrophic interference damage; includes new timing components

problems.recommendation: If your Baja's timing belt history is unknown or overdue, invest in a preventative service immediately. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt engine is typically the only viable path forward.
problems.cta: Compare timing belt service vs rebuilt engine costs for your Baja

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru Baja
finalCta.paragraph: Head gasket, turbo, oil consumption, or timing belt issues don't have to mean the end for your grey import Baja. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Subaru Baja specialists who understand boxer engine compatibility with Legacy and Outback donors. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru Baja Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru Baja and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

years.preview: Pre-Facelift Baja: EJ251/EJ252 Naturally Aspirated Debut

keyChanges: Subaru Baja launched for US market; UK availability via grey import specialists
EJ251 2.5L SOHC naturally aspirated boxer fitted to Base trim
Sport trim introduced mid-cycle with minor EJ252 refinements
AWD standard across all variants, boxer engine layout confirmed
mainEngines: EJ252 - 2.5L petrol naturally aspirated (Sport, late Base)
knownFor: Economical naturally aspirated boxer performance for UK grey import buyers
Head gasket vigilance essential from 80k miles onwards
Strong compatibility with Legacy/Outback EJ25 donor engines for replacements

keyChanges: Facelift styling and interior updates for 2005 model year
EJ255 2.5L turbocharged boxer introduced for Turbo and Turbo Limited trims
Minor ECU and fuel system refinements across naturally aspirated models
Final production year 2006; low-mileage examples now sought after
knownFor: EJ255 turbo delivers 210-224hp for performance and off-road enthusiasts
Turbo models require vigilant servicing to avoid boost/oil consumption issues
Low-mileage 2006 examples command premium in UK grey import market

faq.h2: Subaru Baja Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru Baja engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru Baja engine replacements typically range from £895 for used naturally aspirated units to £3,200 for rebuilt turbo EJ255 engines, supply only. Supply & fit adds £450-£850 depending on location and garage rates.
keyPoints: Naturally aspirated EJ251/EJ252 rebuilt: £895-£2,100 supply only
Turbo EJ255 rebuilt: £1,495-£3,200 supply only
Supply & fit service adds £450-£850 to engine price
All quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Get your free Subaru Baja engine replacement quote today

items.question: Which engine code fits my Subaru Baja?
items.answer: Your Baja uses either EJ251, EJ252 (naturally aspirated) or EJ255 (turbo) depending on trim and year. Enter your registration or VIN above to instantly verify your exact engine code and compatible replacements.
keyPoints: Base models (2002-2006): EJ251 or EJ252 naturally aspirated
Sport trim (2003-2006): EJ252 naturally aspirated
Turbo/Turbo Limited (2005-2006): EJ255 turbocharged
DVLA lookup confirms exact code before ordering replacement

items.question: What is the difference between used and reconditioned Baja engines?
items.answer: Used engines are tested second-hand units from breakers, while reconditioned engines are fully rebuilt with new bearings, seals and upgraded gaskets. Reconditioned units include longer warranties and address common failure points.
keyPoints: Used: £795-£2,400, 6-month warranty typical, mileage varies
Reconditioned: £895-£3,200, 12-month unlimited warranty standard
Reconditioned includes upgraded MLS head gaskets and piston rings
Reconditioned recommended for high-mileage or failure-damaged units
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Subaru Baja engine options

items.question: Can I fit a different EJ engine code in my Baja?
items.answer: Some EJ codes are interchangeable with minor modifications, but ECU, wiring and mount compatibility must be verified first. Use our compatibility checker or speak to a Subaru Baja specialist before ordering a different code.

EJ255 turbo requires matching ECU, injectors and fuel system
Mounting points and AWD compatibility must be confirmed
